assertions.reason: Confirmed the root route defines an errorComponent in packages/web/app/routes/__root.tsx, and a browser-side runtime fault rendered the friendly fallback UI with the message, Try again button, and Go home link.

Navigating to an invalid project route showed an in-page error state (Couldn’t load this project / Project not found) instead of a blank screen, and the dashboard navigation remained usable to recover back to the dashboard. A direct fetch to /api/projects/999999 returned 404 with {"error":"Project not found"}.

frictions.description: No normal user flow reliably triggered the root route error boundary, so I used a temporary browser-session runtime fault to verify the fallback UI and recovery actions without modifying app code.
frictions.resolution: Injected a temporary Date.prototype.toLocaleDateString failure only inside the isolated browser session, captured the fallback UI, then closed the session.
